info.name: AFCON 2027 Ticketing — API
info.description:
Complete API test collection for the AFCON 2027 Ticketing platform.

## Quick Start
1. Import this collection into Postman.
2. Set the `base_url` collection variable to your server (default: `http://localhost/api`).
3. Run **Register User** → check email for OTP → run **Verify OTP** (token auto-captured).
4. All authenticated requests use `{{token}}` which is set automatically.

## Suggested Test Order
```
01 Register User
02 Verify OTP          ← token auto-saved
03 Login               ← token auto-saved
04 Apply for Fan ID    ← fan_id returned instantly (IMMIGRATION_SKIP=true)
05 Get Fan ID Status
06 Get Announcements
07 Forgot Password → Reset Password
08 Logout
```

## Collection Variables
| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `base_url` | `http://localhost/api` | Change to your server URL |
| `token` | _(auto-filled)_ | Bearer token — set after Login, Verify OTP, or Social Login |

variable.description: Base API URL. Change this to your server address (e.g. https://yourdomain.com/api).
Sanctum Bearer token — auto-captured after Login, Verify OTP, or Social Login.

item.name: 01 · Authentication
item.description:
Register, verify email OTP, login, get current user, and logout.

**Flow:** Register → check email for OTP → Verify OTP (token auto-saved) → use authenticated endpoints.

request.description:
Register a new user with role `customer`.

**Rules:**
- `global_role` must be `customer` or `partner` — never `admin` or `sub_admin`
- `is_active` defaults to `true` automatically
- An OTP is sent to the email — run **Verify OTP** next
- `password_confirmation` must match `password`
- `phone` and `registration_source` are optional

**After this:** Check your email for the 6-digit OTP.
Register a new user with role `partner`. Same rules as customer registration.

exec: console.log('✅ Token captured → {{token}} is ready for authenticated requests');
console.warn('⚠️ Token not found — check OTP value or email address');

request.description:
Submit the 6-digit OTP received by email after registration.

**On success:** Returns user object + Bearer token.

⚡ **Auto-capture:** The Tests script saves the token to `{{token}}` automatically — all authenticated requests are immediately ready.

**Rate-limited:** ~5 attempts per minute.
**OTP expiry:** 10 minutes. Use **Resend OTP** if expired.

Resend a fresh OTP when the previous one expired or was not received.

**`purpose` values:**
- `email_verification` — for new account verification after registration
- `password_reset` — for the forgot-password flow

exec: console.warn('⚠️ Login failed — email may not be verified yet. Run Verify OTP first.');

request.description:
Login with email and password.

**Requires:** Email must be verified first (OTP step).

**Returns:** User object + Bearer token.

⚡ **Auto-capture:** Token is automatically saved to `{{token}}` on success.

Returns the authenticated user's core account data.

🔒 **Requires:** Bearer token.

Invalidates the current Bearer token (deletes the Sanctum token from the database).

🔒 **Requires:** Bearer token.

item.name: 02 · Social Login
item.description:
Sign in with Google or Apple — no OTP verification required.

The mobile client completes OAuth on-device and sends only the `id_token` here. The backend verifies it, then finds or creates the user automatically.

## Account matching logic
| Case | Result |
|------|--------|
| New user, first social login | New account created, email auto-verified, `is_active = true` |
| Same social account logs in again | Same user returned (matched via `social_accounts` table) |
| Email-registered user logs in via social (same email) | Social account linked to existing user, email auto-verified |
| Google user tries Apple with same email | Apple linked to same user — both providers work |
| Deactivated account | Blocked with 422 error |

Lookup order: `(provider, provider_user_id)` first → then by `email`.

request.description:
## How to get a Google ID token for Postman testing

1. Open: https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground
2. Click ⚙️ gear icon → enable **Use your own OAuth credentials** → enter `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` from `.env`
3. In Step 1, select scope: `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.email`
4. Click **Authorize APIs** → sign in with your Google account
5. In Step 2, click **Exchange authorization code for tokens**
6. Copy the `id_token` from the response (long JWT string)
7. Paste it as `id_token` in this request body

> **Dev shortcut:** `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` is empty in `.env`, so audience validation is skipped. Any valid Google `id_token` works regardless of which app issued it.

⚡ Token is auto-saved to `{{token}}` on success.

## Why this test token works

The backend checks Apple tokens for:
- ✅ 3-part JWT format
- ✅ `exp` not expired (valid until April 2027)
- ✅ `iss` = `https://appleid.apple.com`
- ✅ `sub` and `email` present
- ❌ **Signature NOT verified** (noted in source code — safe for dev/staging)

## Test token payload
```json
{
  "iss": "https://appleid.apple.com",
  "sub": "000000.testappleuser123.abc",
  "email": "appletest@example.com",
  "name": "Apple Test User",
  "exp": 1808611835
}
```
Token email: `appletest@example.com`

## Generate a custom test token (different email)
Run in terminal:
```bash
node -e "
const h = Buffer.from(JSON.stringify({alg:'RS256',kid:'test'})).toString('base64url');
const p = Buffer.from(JSON.stringify({
  iss:'https://appleid.apple.com',
  sub:'000000.customuser.abc',
  email:'YOUR@email.com',
  name:'Your Name',
  email_verified:true,
  exp:Math.floor(Date.now()/1000)+3600,
  iat:Math.floor(Date.now()/1000)
})).toString('base64url');
console.log(h+'.'+p+'.'+Buffer.from('fakesig').toString('base64url'));
"
```

⚡ Token is auto-saved to `{{token}}` on success.

item.name: 03 · Password Management
item.description:
Forgot password and reset password via OTP email.

**Flow:** Forgot Password → check email for OTP → Reset Password → Login with new password.

request.description:
Sends a 6-digit password-reset OTP to the provided email.

**Security:** Always returns a success message — even if the email does not exist. This prevents email enumeration attacks.

OTP is only sent if the email exists and is verified. After this, run **Reset Password** with the received OTP.

Resets the password using the OTP received via email.

**Fields:**
- `email` — account email address
- `otp` — 6-digit code from the email
- `password` — new password (min 8 characters)
- `password_confirmation` — must match `password`

**OTP expiry:** 10 minutes (set via `OTP_EXPIRY_MINUTES` in `.env`).

After success, login with the new password.
Resend the password-reset OTP if it expired before use.

item.name: 04 · User Profile
item.description:
View and update the authenticated user's profile.

🔒 All requests require Bearer token.

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's full profile.

🔒 **Requires:** Bearer token.

Update the authenticated user's profile details.

🔒 **Requires:** Bearer token.

item.name: 05 · Fan ID Application
item.description:
Apply for a Fan ID using identity/travel documents.

## Flow
```
POST /user/fan-id  →  data saved to user_immigration_details table
                   →  sent to immigration department (TODO: real API)
                   →  IMMIGRATION_SKIP=true → verified instantly
                   →  fan_id generated and saved to users.fan_id
                   →  approval email sent to user
```

## Identity type codes
| Code | Who | Document |
|------|-----|----------|
| `nic` | Tanzanian citizen | National Identity Card |
| `rp` | Tanzania resident | Resident Permit |
| `fn` | Foreign National | Passport (any country) |
| `special_pass` | Diplomat / media / official | Special Pass |

## Status values
| Status | Meaning |
|--------|--------|
| `pending` | Submitted, awaiting immigration verification |
| `verified` | Approved — `fan_id` is populated, approval email sent |
| `rejected` | Not approved — `rejection_reason` set; user may re-apply |

🔒 All requests require Bearer token.

item.name: Apply for Fan ID — NIC (Tanzanian Citizen)

request.description:
Apply using a **National Identity Card (NIC)** — for Tanzanian citizens.

**Required fields:**
| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `full_name` | string | Full legal name as on document |
| `gender` | string | `male` or `female` |
| `date_of_birth` | date `Y-m-d` | Must be a past date |
| `nationality` | string (2 chars) | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. `TZ`, `NG`, `EG`, `CM`) |
| `identity_type` | string | `nic` \| `rp` \| `fn` \| `special_pass` |
| `identity_number` | string | Document number |
| `identity_expiry_date` | date `Y-m-d` | Must be a **future** date |

**Identity type reference:**
- `nic` — Tanzanian citizen (National ID Card)
- `rp` — Tanzania resident (Resident Permit holder)
- `fn` — Foreign National (passport holder, any country)
- `special_pass` — Diplomat, media, or official

**Dev mode (IMMIGRATION_SKIP=true):**
Fan ID is generated instantly. Response includes `fan_id` and `status: verified`.
Approval email is also sent to the user.

**Production mode:**
Status will be `pending` — immigration department must approve.

🔒 Requires: Bearer token.

item.name: Apply for Fan ID — FN (Foreign National)

request.description:
Apply as a **Foreign National (FN)** using a passport — for visitors from any country.

`identity_type` is `fn`. `nationality` is the visitor's 2-letter country code.

🔒 Requires: Bearer token.

item.name: Apply for Fan ID — RP (Resident Permit)

request.description:
Apply using a **Resident Permit (RP)** — for Tanzania residents who hold a work or residence permit.

`identity_type` is `rp`. Nationality is `TZ` as the permit is issued by Tanzania.

🔒 Requires: Bearer token.

Returns the authenticated user's latest Fan ID application.

**Response includes:**
- `fan_id` — generated ID string (populated only after verification)
- `status` — `pending` / `verified` / `rejected`
- `verified_at` — timestamp when approved
- `rejection_reason` — reason if rejected (user may re-apply)
- `submitted_at` — when application was submitted
- Personal + document details submitted
- `immigration_logs` — API call history with immigration department

🔒 Requires: Bearer token.

item.name: 06 · Announcements
item.description:
Public endpoint — no authentication required.

Returns published announcements only. Pinned items appear first.

request.description:
Returns all published announcements ordered by: pinned first → newest `published_at`.

**Pagination:** 15 per page by default.

🔓 **Public** — no token required.

query.description: Filter by type: info | warning | success | danger

request.description:
Returns published announcements filtered by type.

**`type` options:** `info` · `warning` · `success` · `danger`

**`per_page`:** 1–100 (default 15)

🔓 **Public** — no token required.

item.name: 07 · Health Check
item.description: Use to confirm the API server is reachable before testing.

request.description:
Returns `{ status: ok, timestamp: ... }`. Use to confirm the server is running.

🔓 **Public** — no token required.
